questions.text: Which among the following was Rizal's godfather?
Which among the following was not Rizal's brother or sister?
Which among the following isn't part of Rizal's races?
The real surname of the Rizal family was Mercado, which was adopted first by whom?
Which among the stories told by Rizal's mother left a deep impression on Rizal's mind about 'death of a martyr'?
The death of the three priest martyrs awakened Rizal's spirit of patriotism. Which among the following isn't part of the three martyrs?
When Rizal was eleven he was sent to what school to study which was under the supervision of the Jesuits?
Who among the professors in Ateneo did Rizal consider to be the best and also helped Rizal to study harder and improve his poetry?
Rizal went to University of Santo Tomas to seek higher education. Which among the following wasn't Rizal's course on that institution?
What course did Father Pablo Ramon, Ateneo's Rector, advice Rizal to take?
Rizal Completed his medicine course in what university in Spain?
Rizal joined freemasonry as aid against the friars in the Philippines. In which Masonic lodge did Rizal first join?
After completing his studies in Madrid, Rizal went to Paris and Germany to specialize what field in medicine?
When Rizal was in Paris, he worked as an assistant to a famous French doctor named?
After acquiring enough experience in his specialization in Paris, Rizal left for what German city famous for its old university and romantic surroundings?
What interests did Ferdinand Blumentritt, Director of the Ateneo of Leitmeritz, have which ultimately became the reason for his acquaintance with Rizal?
While Rizal was in Germany he translated several famous books into tagalog, one of which was Schiller's William Tell and another was Fairy Tales by a famous writer named?

When Rizal was in Berlin he came in contact with great scientists, one of which was Dr. Feodor Jagor who wrote a famous book, which foretold the downfall of Spanish rule in the Philippines and the coming of America to Philippine shores, entitled what?
Rizal wanted to master French so he took private lessons from a professor named?
Who became the savior of Noli Me Tangere?
Upon arriving in his hometown from abroad, Rizal was called with a certain name for he was a doctor who came from Germany, what was his name?
Governor-General Terrero assigned a young Spanish lieutenant as a bodyguard to Rizal as a security measure. Who was this Spanish lieutenant?
Who was the theologian from the Manila Cathedral who became a gallant defender of Noli?
Rizal befriended a Japanese journalist who wrote two political novels (Nankai-no-Daiharan and O-unabara). What was his name?
What country did Rizal describe as 'the land par excellence of freedom but only for the whites'?
Rizal's greatest achievement while in London was his annotation of Sucesos de las Islas Filipinas written by whom?
While Rizal was in London, he became the honorary president of a patriotic society named what?
During his 2nd sojourn in Paris, Rizal formed a club which was purely social and temporary for young Filipinos. What was the name?
Rizal formed another society which pledged its members to excel in intellectual and physical prowess. What was the name?
Who was Rizal's bitter enemy of the pen who later authored the first book-length biography of the hero?
Who became the saviour of El Filibusterismo?
One of the reasons why Rizal decided to return to Manila was the formation of a society named what?
Rizal conceived the establishment of a Filipino colony in North Borneo (Sabah) which he considered to be as what?
Who was the Governor-General that plotted the 'Spanish Trap' against Rizal?
Which member of the society Rizal founded became the 'real father of revolution'?
Where was Rizal deported due to incriminatory leaflets found on Lucia's pillow cases?
The novel El Filibusterismo was dedicated by Rizal to whom?
According to Wenceslao Retana, what was Rizal's only vice?
The friars hired a spy who assumed what name to incriminate Rizal?
Who was the woman which Rizal considered to be his wife?
Who was the only Spanish priest who went in defense to Rizal's Noli publicly?
Which among the following pen-names did Rizal use in writing La Vision del Fray Rodriguez?
What was the title of Rizal's first article in the patriotic newspaper La Solidaridad?

options.a: Letter to the Young Women of Malolos

questions.text: What was the name of the poem that Rizal submitted for the literary contest held by Liceo Artistico-Literario in 1879?
What was the title of the first poem which Rizal wrote when he was eight years old?
What sculptural masterpiece did Rizal carve for Father Lleonart?
This novel was described as a romantic novel; it is a 'work of the heart' and a 'book of feeling'.
This novel was considered to be a political novel; a 'work of the head' and a 'book of the thought'.
Which among the following isn't part of the species Rizal discovered and named after him?
What is Rizal's literary masterpiece which was considered to be the 'a priceless gem of Philippine Literature'?
Rizal considers Noli Me Tangere as superior to El Filibusterismo
The name 'Jose' was by his mother because she was a devotee of San Juan
Dr. Pio Viola was named emissary to Dapitan, in order to inform Rizal of the plan of the Katipunan to launch a revolution.
Don Pedro and Don Miguel Camus along with other Filipino residents urged Rizal in Singapore to disembark and seek protection of the British law.
Rizal chose Lt. Luis Taviel de Andrade as his defense counsel for his trial.
Governor-General Camilo Polavieja ordered Rizal to be shot at 7:00 o'clock in the morning of December 30 at Laangbayan Field (Luneta).
Jose Rizal named Philippines as the 'Pearl of the Orient Sea'
The Dominican friars helped Polavieja to replace Governor-General Blanco which ultimately sealed the death of Rizal.
Rizal hid his last poem in an alcohol lamp and left it to his sister Trinidad.
Before he was shot, Rizal requested that he be shot facing the firing squad and the request was accepted.
Rizal had a premonition that he will somehow die on December 30 as proved by an entry on his diary on January 1, 1883.
Jose Rizal's first teacher was his mother.
In 1880 the Artistic-Literary Lyceum held a literary contest to commemorate the fourth centennial of the death of Homer where Rizal won.
Rizal was not awarded his Doctor's diploma because he did not present the thesis required for graduation nor paid the corresponding fees.
Ferdinand Blumentritt is considered to be Jose Rizal's bestfriend.
